description: TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) rates applicable for FY 2025-26 for common payment types. TDS credit is reflected in Form 26AS and AIS and can be claimed in ITR.
note: If PAN is not provided by payee, TDS is deducted at 20% (or double the applicable rate, whichever is higher) u/s 206AA. Aadhaar can be used in lieu of PAN for certain transactions.

tdsRates.paymentType: Salary / Wages
tdsRates.threshold: Above basic exemption limit (₹4L new regime / ₹2.5L old regime)
tdsRates.rateNote: Employer deducts TDS at the applicable income tax slab rate for the employee's estimated income. Form 16 issued.

tdsRates.paymentType: Premature withdrawal from EPF (before 5 years of service)
Interest on listed debentures, government securities
Dividend from domestic companies
tdsRates.rateNote: 10% for residents; 20% for non-residents (or DTAA rate if lower)

tdsRates.description: TDS on Interest Other than Securities
tdsRates.paymentType: Bank FD interest, RD interest, company deposit interest, P2P lending
tdsRates.threshold: ₹40,000 per bank per FY (₹50,000 for senior citizens)

tdsRates.paymentType: Lottery prizes, crossword puzzle winnings, card game winnings
Net winnings from online gaming platforms
tdsRates.threshold: No threshold — applies on all net winnings
tdsRates.rateNote: Net winnings = total winnings minus total deposits. TDS at point of withdrawal.

tdsRates.paymentType: Winnings from horse races
Payment to contractors, sub-contractors, catering, advertising, printing
tdsRates.threshold: ₹30,000 per transaction OR ₹1,00,000 in aggregate per year

tdsRates.paymentType: Insurance commission paid to agents
Commission, brokerage paid to agents (not insurance)
Rent for land, building, machinery, plant, equipment
tdsRates.rateNote: Tenant must deduct TDS if annual rent exceeds ₹2,40,000

tdsRates.description: TDS on Purchase of Immovable Property
tdsRates.paymentType: Purchase of immovable property (other than agricultural land)
tdsRates.rateNote: Buyer deducts TDS. Seller claims credit in ITR.

tdsRates.paymentType: Rent paid by individual/HUF not covered by 194I
tdsRates.rateNote: Applicable when monthly rent > ₹50,000. TDS deducted only once (at year-end or last month). Individual/HUF taxpayer need not have tax audit for this TDS obligation.

tdsRates.paymentType: Professional fees (doctors, lawyers, CAs, consultants), technical services, royalty
Cash withdrawals from bank accounts exceeding threshold
tdsRates.threshold: ₹1 Crore per year (₹20L if no ITR filed in last 3 years)
tdsRates.note: TDS credit can be claimed in ITR even for this deduction

tdsRates.paymentType: Purchase of goods (buyer responsible if turnover > ₹10Cr)
tdsRates.threshold: ₹50 lakh from a single seller in a FY

tdsRates.paymentType: Payment on transfer of Virtual Digital Assets (crypto, NFT)
Interest, royalty, FTS, capital gains and other payments to NRIs/foreign companies
tdsRates.rate: Varies: 20% (interest), 10% (royalty/FTS), rate on capital gains; DTAA rates if lower

tdsRates.paymentType: TCS collected by seller on goods sales exceeding ₹50L to a buyer
Remittances under Liberalised Remittance Scheme
tdsRates.rateNote: Rate on LRS remittances above ₹10L per year. This TCS can be credited against income tax liability.
